steps.why: Retrieving Pokedex Ts Catch Reducer Tests through the concrete replayable immutable Pokemon collection reducer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Prove threshold boundaries, duplicate, changed identity, lookup failure, abort, replay, and previous-state immutability.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Catch Reducer Tests precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A lab TypeScript team handles case 5277 during a clean emitted-package smoke project. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile replayable catch decisions with immutable collection state and stable identity; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, and packed artifact; then change one command, token, EOF, abort, or output-width case and preserve the session model. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Prove threshold boundaries, duplicate, changed identity, lookup failure, abort, replay, and previous-state immutability.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: One deterministic success and failure test proves collection behavior.
